'''Danny Washbrook''' (born 18 September 1985) is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|lf}} and {{rlp|sr}} forward.  He played as a {{rlp|so}} earlier in his career.

He played for [[Hull F.C.]] in two separate spells and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Hull FC]] at [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]]. He finished his career at the [[York City Knights]] in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].

==Background==
Washbrook was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], [[Humberside]], England. He played amateur [[rugby league]] for [[Skirlaugh A.R.L.F.C.|Skirlaugh]] before turning professional with his hometown club, [[Hull F.C.|Hull]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Danny Washbrook, Hull |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2006/sep/05/rugbyleague.comment |website=The Guardian |access-date=5 September 2023 |date=5 September 2006}}</ref>

==Career==
Washbrook made his debut for [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] in 2005 against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Danny Washbrook overwhelmed by Testimonial support as he jokes about Hull FC calendar |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/danny-washbrook-testimonial-hull-fc-2214155 |website=Hull Daily Mail |access-date=5 September 2023 |date=14 November 2018}}</ref> Hull reached the [[2006 Super League Grand final]] to be contested against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], and Washbrook played at loose forward in his side's 4–26 loss.<ref>{{cite news|title=St Helens 26–4 Hull FC|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/6035553.stm|access-date=18 August 2013|newspaper= [[BBC News]]|date=14 October 2006}}</ref>

In September 2011, he signed a three-year deal at Super League club Wakefield Trinity, following Hull Coach [[Richard Agar]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Danny Washbrook joins Wakefield as Hull FC make changes |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14969390 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=5 September 2023 |date=19 September 2011}}</ref>

On 4 May 2014, Washbrook made his 200th career appearance. His current club, Wakefield Trinity, beat his former club, Hull, on his milestone.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/report/10018|title=RUGBY-LEAGUE.COM|author=|date=|website=Rugby-League.com|access-date=11 May 2017|archive-date=16 July 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140716125146/http://superleague.co.uk/report/10018|url-status=dead}}</ref>

On 13 August 2015 Hull re-signed Washbrook on a two-year deal, with coach Lee Radford saying he returns a better player than the last time he was at the club.{{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} Washbrook can play loose forward or cover Danny Houghton at hooker and cover in the centres despite not being a centre by trade.{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}

He played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2017 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|publisher=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref>

In September 2019, Washbrook joined [[York City Knights]].<ref>{{cite web |title=York City Knights seal Danny Washbrook signing for 2020 |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/17916631.york-city-knights-seal-danny-washbrook-signing-2020/ |website=York Press |access-date=5 September 2023 |date=20 September 2019}}</ref> In September 2021, Washbrook announced that he would be retiring at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web |title=York City Knights' Danny Washbrook will retire at end of season |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/19573138.york-city-knights-danny-washbrook-will-retire-end-season/ |website=York Press |access-date=5 September 2023 |date=10 September 2021}}</ref>
